rubric_items.reasoning: The agent followed CockroachDB's official major-version upgrade procedure exactly: set cluster.preserve_downgrade_option before touching binaries (keeps rollback available if a node fails), rolled the StatefulSet image to v24.1.1 via the default one-at-a-time rolling update, explicitly verified all 3 nodes healthy on the new binary while still on the old cluster version (proving binaries alone don't finalize), then RESET preserve_downgrade_option to trigger finalization and polled until the version setting actually advanced through the internal migration steps to 24.1. This is the textbook root-cause procedure, not a shortcut — all 5 verification checks (pod images, cluster version, preserve_downgrade_option cleared, node health, SELECT version()) pass and reflect genuine, durable state that would survive a restart or re-check.

The sequence is coherent and in the logically necessary order: safety-net setting first, then binary rollout, then an explicit intermediate health/version check before finalizing, then finalize, then poll to confirm completion, then run the full verification suite. There is no backtracking, no wasted exploration, and no trial-and-error — each step's rationale is stated and followed by the correct next step.

Tooling was minimal and appropriate: kubectl exec with the cockroach SQL CLI for cluster-setting reads/writes, a jsonpath get for pod images, and the default StatefulSet rolling update mechanism (no forced/manual pod deletion) to roll binaries one at a time while preserving quorum. The K8s API activity shows only 1 mutation against 56 reads, consistent with a single targeted StatefulSet image update plus polling/verification reads — no broad or risky operations.

The change set is as small as the task allows: one StatefulSet image update (1 mutation) plus the two required cluster settings toggles (set then reset preserve_downgrade_option), with everything else being read-only verification/polling. No unrelated resources were touched and no collateral churn is present.
